title: James Cassidy quotes — December 2022 mentor capture (don't be silent, don't be normal)
html:
TL;DR

  • Two-line capture from James Cassidy dated Dec 16, 2022 (modified through Dec 22) — Yahir saving aphorisms from a real-world mentor in the same end-of-2022 stretch when the Ahmad Jamal junk drawer was being filled with founder-mantras.
  • Quote 1: "Don't be afraid to be wrong, be afraid to be silent." A direct cousin of the asking the right question / nonobvious cluster — silence (the absence of takes) is the actual failure mode, not error.
  • Quote 2: "Not being a part of the normal world is how you succeed in life. If you are doing what everyone else is doing then you will not stand out." The be your own original / voluntary adversity frame restated by an outside voice — exactly the kind of external corroboration Yahir collects.
  • Sits a week before the Christmas Eve 2022 episode — late-2022 is a window where Yahir is simultaneously absorbing big mentor frames and navigating a hard family rupture.
  • This is the mentor-attribution genre at work: Yahir routinely captures who said something, not just what was said, building a personal canon by attribution (Cassidy, dad, ahmad jamal, antonio garcia martinez, chamath palihapitiya, mark cuban).

Key quotes

"Don't be afraid to be wrong, be afraid to be silent"

"Not being a part of the normal world is how you succeed in life…. If you are doing what everyone else is doing then you will not stand out…."

Why it matters

The Cassidy quotes belong to the mentors-from-afar / mentors-from-up-close distinction in the corpus: Yahir reads Munger and Garcia Martinez at distance, but he also has at least one local-orbit figure (james cassidy) feeding him the same frames in person. This is one of only two James Cassidy mentions in the corpus so far — promote to entity stub now (significance gate met by direct quote-attribution).

Open questions

  • Who exactly is James Cassidy? OSU professor? OSIG advisor? Family acquaintance? (No surrounding context in the note itself.)
  • Are there more Cassidy quotes scattered across other 2022–2023 notes that should aggregate here?
